channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Texas man whose execution was halted by subpoena won't testify in person before lawmakers
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man whose execution was halted after lawmakers ordered Robert Roberson to appear at the state Capitol did not show up as planned Monday after a dispute over transporting a death row inmate from prison for the extraordinary purpose of testifying in a public hearing.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Some Jewish voters in presidential swing states reconsider their longtime devotion to Democrats
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — For Rona Kaufman, the signs are everywhere that more Jews feel abandoned by the Democratic Party and may vote for Republican Donald Trump. It's in her Facebook feed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia investigates the claimed shoot-down of a cargo jet in Sudan's Darfur region
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Forces said Monday they shot down a cargo jet in the country’s far western reaches of Darfur, a claim that Russian diplomats said they were trying to investigate in the war zone.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: North Dakota woman accused of poisoning boyfriend over inheritance to serve 25 years in prison
item.description: The North Dakota woman believed to have killed her boyfriend over an inheritance will serve 25 years behind bars following her sentencing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One of the last remaining WWII Navajo Code Talkers dies at 107
item.description: One of the last remaining Navajo Code Talkers who transmitted messages during World War II using the tribe's native language has died, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: Walmart and Aldi are once again offering meal deals for Thanksgiving
item.description: The two companies are advertising their dinners at "inflation-free" or "pre-inflation" prices, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: PHOTOS: Giant Pumpkin Regatta makes a splash
item.description: Paddlers dressed in their Halloween best raced in carved-out pumpkins with all their might as part of the West Coast Giant Pumpkin Regatta.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, Fox 5

item.title: Who is Lucifer 'Justin Case' Everylove? Meet the presidential candidate who is only on Utah's ballots
item.description: Who is Lucifer "Justin Case" Everylove? Here's how this unaffiliated presidential candidate made it onto the ballots in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 people shown on camera leaving fatally shot teen at Las Vegas hospital
item.description: North Las Vegas police are asking for the public's help to identify two people they say were caught on camera leaving a fatally shot teen in a hospital lobby.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, NewsNation

item.title: Russell Wilson throws for 2 TDs, runs for another TD in Pittsburgh debut as Steelers rout Jets 37-15
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Russell Wilson sat at the podium, uniform still on, in no rush to move on from a moment he believed would always come. Even as the weeks passed and his aching calf struggled to get better. Even as Justin Fields deftly guided the Pittsburgh Steelers in his place.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Royal Caribbean cruise line sued over hidden camera that may have filmed hundreds of passengers: lawsuit
item.description: Arvin Mirasol was sentenced to 30 years for filming children in stages of undress on a Royal Caribbean cruise – a new lawsuit alleges as many as 960 passengers could have been filmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How does abortion translate? Ballot measures are a challenge for interpreters
item.description: Reproductive rights measures are on the ballots in 10 states after heated debates over terminology and impact — and that’s just in English.
item.dc:creator: Michael Warren, Geoff Mulvihill And Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In Nevada, immigration is a nuanced issue. But the politics of it is painted in black and white
item.description: Immigrants, as well as those who work closely with them, say both sides’ efforts to crack down on the border have clouded the larger immigration issue in ways often too nuanced to break down cleanly along ideological lines.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Inflation would be higher under Trump: Economists
item.description: The percentage of economists who believe it rose from the Journal’s last survey, which was before both candidates released their major policy proposals.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Woman goes missing in Maine forest for days, is rescued hundreds of feet from dead husband
item.description: Maine officials announced the rescue of Pamela Helmstadter on Thursday, who went missing with her husband John a few days earlier. John had died after suffering a fall.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Battleground Florida: FEMA funds
item.description: Tampa Bay is working to recover from two recent back-to-back hurricanes that impacted the state weeks away from the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Patrick Mahomes and Chiefs stay undefeated, beat 49ers 28-18 in Super Bowl rematch
item.description: The formula might look a bit different with the defense and running game carrying the Kansas City Chiefs. But the results are awfully familiar as the two-time defending champions keep knocking off teams — especially the San Francisco 49ers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about credit card APR
item.description: From good vs. bad rates to avoiding common pitfalls, credit card APR can significantly impact your financial health.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Thelma Mothershed Wair, a member of the Little Rock Nine who integrated an Arkansas school, has died
item.description: Thelma Mothershed Wair, one of the nine Black students who integrated a high school in Arkansas' capital city of Little Rock in 1957 while a mob of white segregationists yelled threats and insults, has died at age 83.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 Navy aviators are declared dead after a fighter jet crashed in Washington state
item.description: Two crew members who were missing following the crash of a fighter jet in mountainous terrain in Washington state during a routine training flight have been declared dead, the U.S. Navy said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump overtakes Harris for first time in Decision Desk HQ/The Hill election forecast
item.description: CORRECTION: An earlier version of this story misstated the chances of Harris winning the election in the latest forecast from Decision Deck HQ/The Hill. The correct number is 48 percent.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Asheville woman discovers home burglarized after fleeing hurricane
item.description: "They took all [my husband's] power tools that were light and a bunch of different equipment," Jacqui Fehl said.
item.dc:creator: Harrison Grubb, NewsNation

item.title: Mormon church now offering sleeveless garments for members in warmer climates
item.description: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints is now offering sleeveless garments for members in hot and humid climates, according to a Church spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, WGN 9

item.title: 2 Navy aviators declared dead after fighter jet crash in Washington state
item.description: The aviators' names won’t be released until a day after their next of kin have been notified, the Navy said in a statement Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: One of the last Navajo Code Talkers from World War II dies at 107
item.description: WINDOW ROCK, Ariz. (AP) — John Kinsel Sr., one of the last remaining Navajo Code Talkers who transmitted messages during World War II based on the tribe's native language, has died. He was 107. Navajo Nation officials in Window Rock announced Kinsel’s death on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Zealand wins cricket's Women’s T20 World Cup for 1st time with 32-run victory over South Africa
item.description: New Zealand won the Women’s T20 World Cup for the first time, beating South Africa by 32 runs on Sunday after a standout performance from Amelia Kerr with bat and ball.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect escapes Miami hospital after complaining of chest pains, flees in Maserati: police
item.description: Theodric Collins, 36, was evaluated at Jackson Memorial Hospital in Miami before making his escape in a waiting Maserati while still handcuffed, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of frozen waffle products recalled due to possible listeria contamination
item.description: Hundreds of frozen waffle products sold in leading retailers including Walmart and Target are being recalled because of possible contamination by the listeria bacteria, according to the manufacturer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Many gift-buying parents pressured to overspend this holiday season: Survey
item.description: (NerdWallet) - Holiday shopping season is upon us, and the pressure is on, or at least it feels that way for some parents.
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Liam Payne fans pay tribute after singer's tragic death
item.description: Days after former One Direction singer, Liam Payne, died at age 31 while in Argentina, fans all over the world are paying tribute at his vigil in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman needing double-lung transplant marries her donor's brother
item.description: One of the scariest moments turned into something more beautiful than one woman could've ever imagined.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bath & Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath & Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KTLA 5

item.title: Alec Baldwin returns to 'Saturday Night Live' to spoof Bret Baier interview with Kamala Harris
item.description: Alec Baldwin has returned to “Saturday Night Live.” But the actor who won an Emmy for playing former President Donald Trump didn't reprise that role — he returned Saturday night to play Fox News' Bret Baier in an opening skit that spoofed this week's interview with Kamala Harris.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin US Senate candidates trade accusations of lying during testy debate
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Democratic U.S. Sen. Tammy Baldwin and Republican challenger Eric Hovde repeatedly accused each other of lying over the course of their closely watched Senate race during an often-testy and confrontational debate Friday.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, star of ‘South Pacific,’ dies at 93
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: KAITLYN HUAMANI and BOB THOMAS, Associated Press, ABC 4
